content.thinking:
The user is showing me a screenshot of a terminal inside what appears to be a VNC session in their Sandcastle sandbox. The issue is that `chromium-browser` is not installed via apt but requires the snap package. On arm64 Ubuntu, snap chromium is the recommended way, but snaps don't work inside containers.

For the arm64 sandbox image, we need to install Chromium in a way that works without snap (since snap doesn't work in containers). The solution is to install `chromium` directly from the Ubuntu apt repositories (not the snap wrapper).

Let me look at the sandbox image Dockerfile to understand the current setup.

file.content:
FROM ubuntu:25.10

ARG SANDCASTLE_VERSION=dev

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System tools
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    openssh-server sudo curl git tmux vim neovim \
    build-essential \
    jq ripgrep fd-find htop wget unzip ca-certificates net-tools iputils-ping \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GUI tools: TigerVNC (Xvnc = virtual X server + VNC server combined), and browser
# Xvnc sends the RFB banner immediately on connect, unlike x11vnc 0.9.17+ which waits
# for client data first (breaking websockify's server-speaks-first expectation).
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    tigervnc-standalone-server openbox xterm xfonts-base xfonts-100dpi xfonts-75dpi \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Google Chrome (amd64) or Chromium (arm64) — Google doesn't ship a Chrome deb for arm64
ARG TARGETARCH
RUN if [ "$TARGETARCH" = "amd64" ]; then \
      apt-get update \
      && wget -q https://dl.google.com/linux/direct/google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
      && apt-get install -y ./google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
      && rm google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*; \
    else \
      apt-get update \
      && apt-get install -y chromium \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*; \
    fi

# Prefer IPv4 to avoid slow/broken IPv6 connections
RUN sed -i 's/#precedence ::ffff:0:0\/96  100/precedence ::ffff:0:0\/96  100/' /etc/gai.conf

# Docker CLI + daemon (Sysbox makes this safe)
RUN curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh

# Pin runc to 1.1.x — runc 1.2+ added a procfs safety check that fails
# inside sysbox containers (sysbox-fs mounts /proc/sys as separate FUSE)
RUN RUNC_VERSION="v1.1.15" \
    && ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture) \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/releases/download/${RUNC_VERSION}/runc.${ARCH}" \
       -o /usr/bin/runc \
    && chmod +x /usr/bin/runc

# Docker Compose plugin
RUN mkdir -p /usr/local/lib/docker/cli-plugins \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/docker/compose/releases/latest/download/docker-compose-linux-$(uname -m)" \
       -o /usr/local/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose

# Stage mise + Claude Code in /opt/sandcastle/bin (seeded to ~/.local/bin on first boot)
RUN mkdir -p /opt/sandcastle/bin \
    && curl https://mise.run | REDACTED sh \
    && curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash \
    && cp -L /root/.local/bin/claude /opt/sandcastle/bin/claude \
    && rm -rf /root/.local/share/claude /root/.local/bin/claude

# Entire CLI — git observability for AI agent sessions (https://entire.io)
RUN ARCH="$(dpkg --print-architecture)" \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/entireio/cli/releases/latest/download/entire_linux_${ARCH}.tar.gz" \
       | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin entire \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/entire

# Shell config: PATH, mise activation, aliases
RUN echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' >> /etc/bash.bashrc \
    && echo 'eval "$(mise activate bash)"' >> /etc/bash.bashrc \
    && echo "alias yolo='claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# Bake version into the image
RUN echo "${SANDCASTLE_VERSION}" > /etc/sandcastle-version

# SSH configuration (key-only auth) — use drop-in config for Ubuntu 25.10 compatibility
RUN mkdir -p /var/run/sshd /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d \
    && echo "PermitRootLogin no" > /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf \
    && echo "PubkeyAuthentication yes" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf \
    && echo "PasswordAuthentication no" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf

COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh

COPY startchrome.sh /usr/local/bin/google-chrome
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/google-chrome \
    && ln -s /usr/local/bin/google-chrome /usr/local/bin/startchrome

COPY tmux.conf /etc/tmux.conf
COPY gitconfig /etc/gitconfig

LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/thieso2/sandcastle"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="Sandcastle sandbox image — Ubuntu 25.10 with Docker-in-Docker, SSH, and dev tools"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="MIT"

WORKDIR /workspace
EXPOSE 22 5900

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]

     3→# so the browser works inside a Docker/Sysbox container with a VNC virtual display.
     4→# Supports both Google Chrome (amd64) and Chromium (arm64).
     5→

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Sandcastle wrapper for Chrome/Chromium — sets up the display and required flags
# so the browser works inside a Docker/Sysbox container with a VNC virtual display.
# Supports both Google Chrome (amd64) and Chromium (arm64).

if [ -x /opt/google/chrome/google-chrome ]; then
    CHROME_BIN=/opt/google/chrome/google-chrome
elif [ -x /usr/bin/chromium ]; then
    CHROME_BIN=/usr/bin/chromium
else
    echo "[sandcastle] No Chrome or Chromium found" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "[sandcastle] browser wrapper: DISPLAY=:99, binary=$CHROME_BIN" >&2

mkdir -p /tmp/chrome-config /tmp/chrome-cache

export REDACTED
export XDG_CACHE_HOME=/tmp/chrome-cache

exec "$CHROME_BIN" \
  --display=:99 \
  --no-sandbox \
  --disable-dev-shm-usage \
  --disable-gpu \
  --disable-software-rasterizer \
  "$@"

content.text:
The issue is that on Ubuntu (since 19.10), `apt install chromium` installs a snap transition package — there's no real binary in containers where snap can't run.

The fix is to use the `xtradeb/apps` PPA which provides real `.deb` Chromium packages. I'll also force the `noble` codename since xtradeb may not have `plucky` (25.10) packages yet.

input.old_string:
else \
      apt-get update \
      && apt-get install -y chromium \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*; \
    fi
input.new_string:
else \
      # Ubuntu ships chromium as a snap-only wrapper since 19.10 — unusable in containers.
      # Use xtradeb/apps PPA which provides a real .deb. Force noble suite since
      # xtradeb may not yet have packages for the current Ubuntu release (25.10/plucky).
      apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends software-properties-common \
      && add-apt-repository -y ppa:xtradeb/apps \
      && CODENAME=$(lsb_release -cs) \
      && find /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ -name "xtradeb*" -exec \
           sed -i "s/\b$CODENAME\b/noble/g" {} \; \
      && apt-get update \
      && apt-get install -y chromium \
      && apt-get purge -y --auto-remove software-properties-common \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*; \
    fi

FROM ubuntu:25.10

ARG SANDCASTLE_VERSION=dev

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System tools
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    openssh-server sudo curl git tmux vim neovim \
    build-essential \
    jq ripgrep fd-find htop wget unzip ca-certificates net-tools iputils-ping \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GUI tools: TigerVNC (Xvnc = virtual X server + VNC server combined), and browser
# Xvnc sends the RFB banner immediately on connect, unlike x11vnc 0.9.17+ which waits
# for client data first (breaking websockify's server-speaks-first expectation).
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    tigervnc-standalone-server openbox xterm xfonts-base xfonts-100dpi xfonts-75dpi \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Google Chrome (amd64) or Chromium (arm64) — Google doesn't ship a Chrome deb for arm64
ARG TARGETARCH
RUN if [ "$TARGETARCH" = "amd64" ]; then \
      apt-get update \
      && wget -q https://dl.google.com/linux/direct/google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
      && apt-get install -y ./google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
      && rm google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*; \
    else \
      apt-get update \
      && apt-get install -y chromium \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*; \
    fi

# Prefer IPv4 to avoid slow/broken IPv6 connections
RUN sed -i 's/#precedence ::ffff:0:0\/96  100/precedence ::ffff:0:0\/96  100/' /etc/gai.conf

# Docker CLI + daemon (Sysbox makes this safe)
RUN curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh

# Pin runc to 1.1.x — runc 1.2+ added a procfs safety check that fails
# inside sysbox containers (sysbox-fs mounts /proc/sys as separate FUSE)
RUN RUNC_VERSION="v1.1.15" \
    && ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture) \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/releases/download/${RUNC_VERSION}/runc.${ARCH}" \
       -o /usr/bin/runc \
    && chmod +x /usr/bin/runc

# Docker Compose plugin
RUN mkdir -p /usr/local/lib/docker/cli-plugins \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/docker/compose/releases/latest/download/docker-compose-linux-$(uname -m)" \
       -o /usr/local/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/lib/docker/cli-plugins/docker-compose

# Stage mise + Claude Code in /opt/sandcastle/bin (seeded to ~/.local/bin on first boot)
RUN mkdir -p /opt/sandcastle/bin \
    && curl https://mise.run | REDACTED sh \
    && curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash \
    && cp -L /root/.local/bin/claude /opt/sandcastle/bin/claude \
    && rm -rf /root/.local/share/claude /root/.local/bin/claude

# Entire CLI — git observability for AI agent sessions (https://entire.io)
RUN ARCH="$(dpkg --print-architecture)" \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/entireio/cli/releases/latest/download/entire_linux_${ARCH}.tar.gz" \
       | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin entire \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/entire

# Shell config: PATH, mise activation, aliases
RUN echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' >> /etc/bash.bashrc \
    && echo 'eval "$(mise activate bash)"' >> /etc/bash.bashrc \
    && echo "alias yolo='claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'" >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# Bake version into the image
RUN echo "${SANDCASTLE_VERSION}" > /etc/sandcastle-version

# SSH configuration (key-only auth) — use drop-in config for Ubuntu 25.10 compatibility
RUN mkdir -p /var/run/sshd /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d \
    && echo "PermitRootLogin no" > /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf \
    && echo "PubkeyAuthentication yes" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf \
    && echo "PasswordAuthentication no" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-sandcastle.conf

COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh

COPY startchrome.sh /usr/local/bin/google-chrome
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/google-chrome \
    && ln -s /usr/local/bin/google-chrome /usr/local/bin/startchrome

COPY tmux.conf /etc/tmux.conf
COPY gitconfig /etc/gitconfig

LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/thieso2/sandcastle"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="Sandcastle sandbox image — Ubuntu 25.10 with Docker-in-Docker, SSH, and dev tools"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="MIT"

WORKDIR /workspace
EXPOSE 22 5900

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]

lines: +      # Ubuntu ships chromium as a snap-only wrapper since 19.10 — unusable in containers.
+      # Use xtradeb/apps PPA which provides a real .deb. Force noble suite since
+      # xtradeb may not yet have packages for the current Ubuntu release (25.10/plucky).

file.content:
# Google Chrome (amd64) or Chromium (arm64) — Google doesn't ship a Chrome deb for arm64
ARG TARGETARCH
RUN if [ "$TARGETARCH" = "amd64" ]; then \
      apt-get update \
      && wget -q https://dl.google.com/linux/direct/google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
      && apt-get install -y ./google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
      && rm google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb \
      && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*; \
    else \
      # Ubuntu ships chromium as a snap-only wrapper since 19.10 — unusable in containers.
      # Use xtradeb/apps PPA which provides a real .deb. Force noble suite since
      # xtradeb may not yet have packages for the current Ubuntu release (25.10/plucky).
      apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends software-properties-common \
      && add-apt-repository -y ppa:xtradeb/apps \
      && CODENAME=$(lsb_release -cs) \
      && find /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ -name "xtradeb*" -exec \
           sed -i "s/\b$CODENAME\b/noble/g" {} \; \
      && apt-get update \
      && apt-get install -y chromium \
      && apt-get purge -y --auto-remove software-properties-common \

content.text:
Looks good. The `startchrome.sh` wrapper already checks `/usr/bin/chromium` which is exactly where xtradeb installs it, so no changes needed there.

**What changed and why:**

- **Root cause:** On Ubuntu ≥19.10, `apt install chromium` installs a stub shell script that just says "run `snap install chromium`". Since snap requires a systemd-managed host, it can't work inside a container.
- **Fix:** [xtradeb/apps PPA](https://launchpad.net/~xtradeb/apps) ships real `.deb` builds of Chromium for Ubuntu. `add-apt-repository` handles the GPG key import automatically.
- **Codename pinning:** The `sed` step rewrites the generated sources entry from `plucky` → `noble`, since xtradeb typically ships for LTS releases. The noble (24.04) packages are binary-compatible enough with 25.10.

Rebuild the arm64 image and `chromium-browser` / `google-chrome` (via the `startchrome.sh` wrapper) will work.
